---
title: "Support"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Course template note
This is a self-paced course with no instructor and no live support channel. The options below are how to get unstuck on your own.

If you are adapting this course for your institution and *do* have a teaching team, fork the repo and replace this page with your own forum / office-hours / contact info.
:::

## When you get stuck

If a project or exercise is not working, work through this list before reaching out anywhere:

1. **Re-read the relevant page.** Most "stuck" moments are an invitation to look at the reading again. The week pages list which conceptual page covers each topic.
2. **Try the knowledge check** for that week. If you can't answer the questions, the gap is the reading, not the project.
3. **Check the [Glossary](reference/glossary.qmd) and the [Prompt library](reference/prompt-library.qmd)** if a term or pattern is unfamiliar.
4. **Talk to an AI assistant about being stuck**, but use the [Description](fluency/description.qmd) framing — describe what you've tried, what failed, and what you expected. Vague "I'm stuck" prompts produce vague help.

If after that you still can't move, the issue is more likely a code/environment problem than a learning problem — see Technical issues below.

## Technical issues

- For issues with this website (typos, broken links, wrong info), [open an issue](https://github.com/mdmanurung/ai-fluency-for-bio/issues) or a pull request on the course repository.
- For issues with Claude, ChatGPT, or other AI tools: check the vendor's status page first.
- For issues running the [scRNA-seq modules](modules/module-01-raw-data-qc/README.md) — Colab quotas, Cell Ranger errors, Scanpy version mismatches — the most useful first move is to confirm the [Python starter](weeks/starter-week3-python.qmd) loads PBMC 3k. If it doesn't, the module won't either; debug the starter first.
- For other technical questions, the GitHub repository's Issues tab is the canonical place — even if you don't get a maintainer reply, future learners will find your issue.

## Working with peers (optional)

If you'd like to take this course with peers (a small study group of 3–5 works well), the [How to use this course](course-how-to-use.qmd) page has a short note on how to organise that. There is no official forum, no facilitator guide, no answer key beyond what's already in the page — fork the repo, run it your way.

## Wellness

AI tooling is changing quickly, and it's easy to feel like you're "falling behind." You are not. Treat this course as a structured way to develop taste, not a sprint. There are no deadlines — pick a pace you can sustain, and if you stop for a month, just resume from the week page where you left off.
